characters.name: A Christmas Carol (Book)
characters.description: A Christmas Carol in Prose, Being a Ghost-Story of Christmas, most simply referred to asA Christmas Carol,is a 1837 novel written by author Charles Dickens. Centered on an old miser named Ebenezer Scrooge, he is visited by t he ghost of his deceased business partner, Jacob Marley who sends the Ghosts of Christmas Past, Present, and Future who show him the true meaning of Christmas and turns him into a kinder and gentler man.

characters.name: Aunt Marla (A Carol Christmas)
characters.description: Aunt MarlaBehind the ScenesDebut AppearanceA Carol ChristmasCharacter InformationProfessionCarol's ManagerNationalityAmericanFamilyCarol CartmanandBeth Cartman(nieces)Lindsey (great-niece)Tyler (great-nephew)StatusDeceasedCauseOld AgeGALLERYAunt Marla is a character inA Carol Christmas, where she is the aunt ofCarolandBeth Cartmanand great-aunt of Lindsy and Tyler. However, it is never specified if she is the sister of the Cartman sisters' father or their mother. Although teaching her niece to be selfish, in the afterlife, Marla realized the error of her ways and visited Carol and tells her that she will end up like her in the after life if she does not change her ways. Instead, she will send three spirits to change her life.

characters.name: Bailey Pickett
characters.description: Bailey PickettBehind the ScenesDebut AppearanceA London CarolPortrayed ByDebbie RyanCharacter InformationFull NameBailey P ickettSpeciesHumanGenderFemaleAlignmentGoodProfessionStudentNationalityAmericanAge17FamilyEunice Pickett (mother)Clyde Pickett (father)RelationshipsCody Martin(Future husband)FriendsLondon TiptonCody Martin(boyfriend)StatusAliveAdditional AppearancesThe Suite Life on DeckThe Suite Life MovieGALLERY Bailey Pickettis a main character in The Suite Life on Deck. Appearing in all episodes, Bailey is adapted asFredin the episodeA London Carol, where she calls London out on her selfishness and tries pushing her to give to those in need. Instead, London continues to be selfish. However, visited by her mirror when seeing the future, London sees that her continuing mistreatment of Bailey will cause their friendship to dissolve. Instead, London changes her attitude towards Bailey.

characters.name: Belle's Husband
characters.description: Belle's husband (born in 1793)was the second love of Belle. She married him sometime after her breaking her engagement withEbenezer Scrooge. They had many children, one of which was adaughterwho looked just like Belle.

characters.description: The dog in a christmas carol 2009 was a dog that was scared of ebenezer scrooge because the dog could sense and see that he was evil because dog's have that ability to know when someone is evil that they come across by.

characters.name: Ebenezer Scrooge
characters.description: Ebenezer ScroogeBehind the ScenesDebut AppearanceA Christmas Carol(1943)Portrayed BySee "Portrayals"Character InformationFull NameEbenezer ScroogeAliasScroogeMr. ScroogeSpeciesHumanGenderMaleAlignmentGoodProfessionMoney lender and miserNationalityBritishResides InLondon, EnglandAgeLate 50sFamilyUnnamed FatherUnnamed MotherFan Scrooge(younger sister, older in some versions)Unnamed brother-in-law)Fred(nephew)-Fred's Wife(niece-in-law)RelationshipsBelle (ex-fiancé)FriendsSpirit of Christmas Past,Spirit of Christmas Yet to Come,Spirit of Christmas Present,Bob Cratchit,Tiny Tim Cratchit, Cratchit siblings (Martha,Belinda,Peter,Matthew,Lucy),Jacob Marley,Fred,Fred's wifeEnemiesSpirit of Christmas Yet to Come(briefly)AffiliationsScrooge familyStatusAliveGALLERYEbenezer Scroogeis the anti-heroic main protagonist of the novella,A Christmas Carolby Charles Dickens Ebenezer Scrooge was unloved by his father for unknown reasons, but loved dearly by his sister, Fan. She later comes to pick up her brother from boarding school, promising to never have him come back from that awful place. At the beginning of the story, Scrooge is embittered, selfish and downright mean, even not allowing Bob Cratchit to use the coal to warm up the building during the winter. He is even cold towards his nephew, which is never explicitly stated why. The book and especially the 1951 adaptation implies it was because Ebenezer blamed Fred for his sister's death.

characters.name: Emily Cratchit
characters.description: Emily CratchitCharacter informationAlias(es)Emily Cratchit(in most versions)Ethel Cratchit(2022)SpeciesHumanNationalityEnglishOccupation(s)HousewifeSpouse(s)Bob CratchitChild(ren)Martha,Belinda,Lucy(or Gillian)Cratchit(daughters)Peter,Matthew,Timothy "Tiny Tim" Cratchit(sons)StatusAliveGALLERYMrs. Cratchitis the wife of Ebenezer Scrooge's lowly paid clerk, Bob Cratchit and the mother of their many children. In the  new timeline, Emily’s son survives due to Scrooge raising Cratchit's salary. In an unaltered future, Tiny Tim dies, as do Scrooge if the past remains unchanged. She sadly reminscents on her son, and her husband, Robert takes long walks, walking slower since Tiny Tim passed away.

characters.name: Fred
characters.actor_name: Barry MacKay (1938)Michael Medwin (Scrooge, 1970)Dennis Day (The Stingiest Man in Town)Donald Duck (Mickey's Christmas Carol)Roger Rees (1984)Steven Mackintosh (The Muppet Christmas Carol)Dominic West (1999)Julian Ovenden (2004)Colin Firth (2009)
characters.description: FredBehind the ScenesDebut AppearanceA Christmas CarolPortrayed ByBarry MacKay (1938)Michael Medwin (Scrooge, 1970)Dennis Day (The Stingiest Man in Town)Donald Duck (Mickey's Christmas Carol)Roger Rees (1984)Steven Mackintosh (The Muppet Christmas Carol)Dominic West (1999)Julian Ovenden (2004)Colin Firth (2009)Character InformationFull NameFredAliasFred Scrooge (various adaptations)Fred Hollywell (1984, 2009)Harry (1970)Harry Huffam (2022)SpeciesHumanGenderMaleAlignmentGoodNationalityBritishResides InEngland, LondonFamilyUnnamed fatherFan Scrooge(mother, deceased)Ebenezer Scrooge(maternal uncle)Unnamed wifeUnnamed maternal grandmother (deceased)Unnamed maternal grandfather (deceased)FriendsEbenezer Scroogehis wifeBob CratchitAffiliationsScrooge familySkillsKindness and OptimismStatusAliveAdditional AppearancesA Christmas Carol (1910 film)Scrooge (1935 film)Scrooge (1951 film)A Christmas Carol (2009 film)VariousA Christmas CaroladaptationsGALLERYFredis a minor character inA Christmas Caroland the many adaptations.  He is the only sororal nephew and last living relative of the novel's main character Ebenezer Scrooge   and he is the son of Scrooge's late sister, Fan.  Fred's mother, Fan was the sister ofEbenezer Scroogeand died when Fred was young. The reason why Scrooge never wants to be around Fred is because his nephew reminds him of his late sister. Every year, Fred goes to invite his uncle to his Christmas parties and every year, Scrooge declines.

characters.name: Jacob Marley
characters.description: Jacob MarleyOne of the earliest depictions of the character- drawn by John LeechFeatured booksA Christmas CarolFeature filmsSee"Role in adaptations"Character informationFull nameJacob MarleyAliasMr MarleyOccupationMoneylenderAffiliationsScrooge & MarleyGoalTo make Scrooge see the error of his ways so he doesn't share a similar fate to him. (succeeded)RelativesNone knownAlliesEbenezer ScroogeSpirit of Christmas PastSpirit of Christmas PresentSpirit of Christmas Yet to ComeFateDied 1836 ( early 50s )Jacob Marleyis a minor but important character inA Christmas Caroland leads his best friend and partner, Ebenezer on the path of redemption. Spirit of Christmas PastSpirit of Christmas PresentSpirit of Christmas Yet to Come Seven years prior toA Christmas Carol, Jacob dies of unknown circumstances with his heir beingEbenezer Scrooge, his friend, and business partner. Marley is the first ghost (other than the three spirits) to visit Ebenezer seven years later. He first preys upon Scrooge by appearing as the knocker and also causing the bells in the house to ring. Marley appears to Scrooge, warning him to change his ways before he ends up like Marley and the other ghosts they once knew. He says that he will send three spirits, and to expect the first ghost when the bell tolls one, the second at two the next night and the third one at midnight.

Despite living in a small house due to Scrooge not paying Cratchit enough money, Lucy is still happy with the family she has.

characters.name: Matthew Cratchit
characters.description: Matthew CratchitCharacter informationFull nameMatthew CratchitSpeciesMiranda Cratchit (in some versions)NationalityEnglishParent(s)Bob and Emily CratchitSibling(s)Martha, Peter, Belinda, Lucy, TimAlliesTiny Tim Cratchit,Belinda Cratchit,Martha Cratchit,Ebenezer Scrooge,Peter CratchitEnemiesEbenezer Scrooge(formerly)GALLERYMatthew Cratchitis the son ofBob CratchitandEmily Cratchit, the brother ofMartha,Belinda,Peter,Lucy, andTimothy "Tiny Tim" Cratchit. Matthew is the second-youngest boy of the Cratchit family. Despite his father's small pay, Matthew has a happy family life and appears to have a healthy relationship with his siblings and parents. In some versions, he is changed to a girl and named "Miranda."

characters.name: Mr. Fezziwig
characters.description: Mr. Fezziwigis a character from Charles Dickens'A Christmas Carol. He isMrs. Fezziwig's husband. He is portrayed as a generous and jolly man saying things like "Hiliho" and "Chirrup" and the employer of Scrooge.

characters.name: Mrs. Fezziwig
characters.description: Mrs. Fezziwigis the wife of Mr. Fezziwig. She was seen in the novella dancing with her husband during the Christmas Party.

characters.name: Tiny Tim Cratchit
characters.actor_name: Phillip Frost(1935 film,Scrooge)Terry Kilburn(1938 film)Glyn Dearman(1951 film,Scrooge)Timothy Chasin(1977)Dick Billingsly(1983,Mickey's Christmas Carol)Anthony Walters(1984 film)Jerry Nelson(1992; as Robin the Frog inThe Muppet Christmas Carol)Ben Tibber(1994)Jacob Moriarty(A Christmas Carol: The Musical)
characters.description: Tiny Tim CratchitCharacter informationFull nameTimothy CratchitAlias(es)Tiny TimSpeciesHumanNationalityBritishAge7Affiliation(s)Cratchit familyParent(s)Bob Cratchit (father)Emily Cratchit (mother)Sibling(s)Martha Cratchit (sister)Belinda Cratchit (sister)Peter Cratchit (brother)Lucy Cratchit (sister)Matthew Cratchit (brother)AlliesEbenezer Scrooge,Bob,Martha,Belinda,Peter,Lucy, andMatthew CratchitEnemiesEbenezer Scrooge(formerly)AppearancesPortrayed byPhillip Frost(1935 film,Scrooge)Terry Kilburn(1938 film)Glyn Dearman(1951 film,Scrooge)Timothy Chasin(1977)Dick Billingsly(1983,Mickey's Christmas Carol)Anthony Walters(1984 film)Jerry Nelson(1992; as Robin the Frog inThe Muppet Christmas Carol)Ben Tibber(1994)Jacob Moriarty(A Christmas Carol: The Musical)Voiced byRichard Beaumont(1970 film,Scrooge)Ryan Ochoa(2009 film)GALLERYTimothy "Tiny Tim" Cratchitis the youngest son ofBobandEmily Cratchit, and younger brother ofMartha,Belinda,Peter,Lucy, andMatthew Cratchit. He is a minor but important character inA Christmas Carol, andone of the key characters that lead the story's protagonist,Ebenezer Scrooge, on his path towards his redemption.
